steps.title: Identify the structure of the passage
steps.content: The opening sentence states a general trend: every crew member believes he is doing all the work. The underlined sentence then zooms in on Harris specifically — his version of this conviction. The sentence after the underlined one shows George doing the same thing. The underlined sentence sits between a general claim and a parallel second example, which means it must be functioning as the first specific illustration of that general trend.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — It establishes Harris’s feelings about the narrator’s idea of how boat trips usually proceed.: Harris's conviction is about who did the work, not a reaction to the narrator's idea about boat trips in general. ✗
  • Choice B — It suggests that Harris is making an effort to help resolve a conflict within the group.: Harris is not resolving anything — he is convinced he alone worked while the others slacked. ✗
  • Choice C — It indicates that Harris finds his experiences on the boat to be puzzling.: Nothing in the sentence suggests confusion; Harris has a firm, "cast-iron" type of conviction. ✗
  • Choice D — It offers Harris’s belief as a specific example of a trend the narrator has observed while boating.: Harris exemplifies the general trend stated in the first sentence, and George exemplifies it in the next — the underlined sentence is the first specific instance. ✓
